original_text:
### Bolding Rules **[GENERAL — calibrated to the guide's callout/chart patterns]**
- Bold is for **hierarchy and key takeaways, not decoration**. Per email section, bold at most: the heading(s), one key phrase or claim per paragraph, and the statistical callout figure.
- Always bold: callout text per the callout specs (§1.7), statistical callout numbers, brand name within a benefit claim when it is the focal point of the sentence (first instance per section only).
- Never bold: entire paragraphs, footnotes/references, more than ~15% of running body text in any section, prepositional filler, the generic name.
- Never use bold + ALL CAPS + color emphasis simultaneously on the same string except in chart headlines and the campaign headline lockup. Pick at most two emphasis devices per element.
- No underlining except real hyperlinks. No italic for emphasis in patient copy — reserve italics for study names, notes ("Note:"), and citations.

relations.note: Mandatory targets define which specific elements must be bolded within the overall 15% text budget.

quotes: Bold is for **hierarchy and key takeaways, not decoration**
Per email section, bold at most: the heading(s), one key phrase or claim per paragraph, and the statistical callout figure.
Never bold: entire paragraphs, footnotes/references, more than ~15% of running body text in any section

rules.intent: Maintain typographic hierarchy and readability by avoiding over-emphasis.
rules.rule_text: Bold text is reserved for hierarchy and key takeaways, not decoration. Per email section, bold at most: the heading(s), one key phrase or claim per paragraph, and the statistical callout figure. Never bold entire paragraphs, footnotes/references, or more than ~15% of the running body text in any section.

quotes: Always bold: callout text per the callout specs (§1.7), statistical callout numbers, brand name within a benefit claim when it is the focal point of the sentence (first instance per section only).
Never bold: ... prepositional filler, the generic name.

rules.intent: Ensure mandatory visual prominence for key product claims, stats, and critical brand mentions.
rules.rule_text: Always bold callout text, statistical callout numbers, and the brand name within a benefit claim on its first instance per section when it is the sentence focal point. Never bold the generic name or prepositional filler.

quotes: Never use bold + ALL CAPS + color emphasis simultaneously on the same string except in chart headlines and the campaign headline lockup. Pick at most two emphasis devices per element.

rules.intent: Avoid visual clutter and over-styling inside core layout blocks.
rules.rule_text: Never use bold + ALL CAPS + color emphasis simultaneously on the same string, allowing a maximum of two emphasis devices per element. Chart headlines and the campaign headline lockup are exempt from this limit.

quotes: No underlining except real hyperlinks. No italic for emphasis in patient copy — reserve italics for study names, notes ("Note:"), and citations.

rules.intent: Avoid confusing users regarding hyperlink affordances and maintain clear, accessible reading patterns.
rules.rule_text: Do not underline any text except real active hyperlinks. Do not use italic styling for emphasis in patient copy; reserve italics exclusively for study names, notes ("Note:"), and citations.
